title: Al-Qaeda linked insurgents call on Malians to rise up and establish Sharia law
summary: Al-Qaeda affiliated JNIM insurgent group issued a call for Malians to rise against the military government and establish Sharia law, amid ongoing security crisis and reported setbacks for the junta's military forces.
contents:
## Al-Qaeda Affiliate Escalates Insurgency in Mali

### Development
The Group for the Support of Islam and Muslims (JNIM), an Al-Qaeda linked insurgent coalition, issued a public call for the Malian population to rise against the ruling military junta and establish governance under Sharia law.

### Security Context
- Mali's military government has faced increasing pressure from JNIM across northern and central regions
- The withdrawal of French forces and the UN MINUSMA peacekeeping mission left security gaps
- Russian Wagner Group / Afrika Korps forces supporting the junta have suffered setbacks
- Recent fighting around Kidal highlighted the fragility of the military government's control

### Regional Implications
- The Sahel security crisis continues to destabilize West Africa
- Neighboring countries including Burkina Faso and Niger face similar insurgent threats
- The Gulf of Guinea maritime domain may experience spillover effects from regional instability
- International humanitarian organizations report growing displacement

### Threat Assessment
JNIM's public messaging campaign signals confidence in its operational capabilities and represents an escalation in the broader Sahel insurgency. The call to popular uprising suggests the group seeks to undermine the junta's legitimacy beyond purely military means.

### Source Attribution
AFP reporting via Yahoo News World section.
